carbon_policy_signal.item: EU–UK ETS linking negotiations postponed following UK political transition; bilateral agreement timeline extended into H2 2026 or beyond.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: Delayed linkage prolongs UKA-EUA arbitrage uncertainty and free-allocation hedging horizon; extends bilateral carbon-curve shape asymmetry, affecting power-generation marginal cost hedging for multi-year contracts.

freshness_caveat: Coal data stale (182 days, 2025-12-26) and clean-dark stale (177 days, 2025-12-31); fuel-switch and dark-spread economics rely on outdated coal benchmarks. TTF, DE Power, GB Power, EUA, Renewables, Storage current as of 2026-06-25 to 2026-06-26.
risk_flags: Clean Spark 96th percentile (59.64 EUR/MWh) signals unsustainable thermal margin; nuclear cooling risk in France extends horizon beyond intraday heat spikes.
Storage at 47.43% (14.6 pp below seasonal) with only 2.4% filled on 2-day basis; H2 refill pace will determine Q3–Q4 thermal call.
Hormuz shipping disruption and UAE OPEC+ exit tighten LNG spare capacity; crude-to-gas arb may support higher TTF floor despite weak gas demand.
Coal data stale (182 days old, 7th percentile) and clean-dark stale (177 days); fuel-switch economics unclear without current coal prices.

base.summary: Heat wave sustains peak demand; storage refill slow; Hormuz tensions contained; TTF drifts ±3%, DE power ±5%.

downside.summary: European demand softens mid-week; cooling load subsides; Hormuz corridor reopens; methane cap deferral dampens supply-side cost. Storage inflow accelerates.

upside.summary: Hormuz escalation halts LNG shipments or forces reroute; crude spikes; arb-driven gas demand offsets methane-delay weakness. Heat extends into July.

themes: Extreme heat driving thermal scarcity and cooling constraints
Geopolitical crude/LNG upside offsetting weak gas fundamentals
Storage deficit extending power-to-gas switching pressure
EU–UK carbon linkage delay prolongs ETS arbitrage uncertainty
top_drivers: Record European heat wave driving cooling demand, nuclear water-constraint risk, and intraday power volatility; Clean Spark at 96th percentile reflects unsustainable thermal scarcity.
EU storage deficit (14.6 pp below seasonal, 22nd percentile) locks in structural H2 thermal call; refill pace in Jun–Jul will set Q3–Q4 gas-to-power switching regime.
Hormuz shipping tensions + UAE OPEC+ exit tighten LNG spare capacity and support crude/Brent; higher crude-to-gas arb indirectly floors TTF despite weak gas demand.
Methane cap delay and EU–UK ETS linkage postponement extend regulatory uncertainty; lower compliance-cost expectations weaken TTF supply-side floor but keep policy tail-risk alive.
top_takeaway: Clean Spark at 96th percentile amid record heat and Hormuz tensions; storage 14.6 pp below seasonal lows creates sustained thermal scarcity into H2.

watchlist.why: Postponed negotiations delay bilateral carbon market integration; extends UKA-EUA arbitrage and free-allocation hedging uncertainty for next 3–6 months.
Record heat and cooling constraints may force unplanned outages or derate; each GW offline lifts CA+1 power premium by ~5–8 EUR/MWh.
Escalation risk could disrupt LNG flows to northwest Europe; higher Brent (crude-to-gas arb) indirectly supports power-to-gas switching on margin compression.
Delayed restrictions weaken supply-side cost floor; lower expected compliance costs reduce upward pressure on TTF curve but extend regulatory uncertainty into H2 2026.
At 22nd percentile; if summer demand remains elevated or inflow slows, H2 structural gas tightness will force higher power dispatch on thermal and carbon cost.

watchlist_dated.name: EU–UK ETS summit rescheduled (date TBD)
watchlist_dated.why: New date announcement will clarify bilateral carbon linkage timeline and UKA arbitrage hedging window.
